design_philosophy: Precision drafting table meets creative flow. Avoids soft glassmorphism and purple 'tech' gradients. Uses physical, brutalist-inspired toolbars that feel like metal stencils on paper.

ui_font.usage: All UI toolbars, floating panels, property labels, and keyboard shortcuts.

canvas_font.usage: The actual text drawn on the canvas to match the sketchy Rough.js style.

toolbar_button.base_classes: h-10 w-10 flex items-center justify-center border border-border_hard bg-surface_panel transition-transform duration-100

floating_container.base_classes: bg-surface_panel border border-border_hard rounded-none flex items-center shadow-[4px_4px_0px_var(--tw-shadow-color)] shadow-shadow_brutalist

property_slider.thumb: w-4 h-4 bg-surface_panel border-2 border-border_hard rounded-none

keyboard_shortcut_indicator.classes: absolute bottom-1 right-1 text-[10px] text-text_secondary font-ui_font opacity-60

micro_interactions.button_click: Active scale-95 translation to reset brutalist shadow
micro_interactions.panel_entrance: Framer motion, slide in from nearest edge, stiff spring physics (no soft easing)

testing_attributes.rules: All interactive and key informational elements MUST include a `data-testid` attribute.

instructions_to_main_agent: Do not use Shadcn card components; construct floating toolbars using the `floating_container` and `toolbar_button` brutalist classes provided.
Implement the canvas logic using React and Rough.js (or similar) to achieve the sketchy drawing feel.
Apply `data-testid` meticulously to every button and tool in the UI.
Load 'Shantell Sans' and 'IBM Plex Mono' from Google Fonts in the index.html or global css.
Map keydown events (e.g., '1' for selection, '2' for pen, 'R' for rectangle) and display them using the `keyboard_shortcut_indicator`.
